assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intercompany receivable/payable.
The entities involved have different functional currencies, creating foreign exchange exposure in the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a possible hedged item, making fair value hedge accounting a relevant top-level hedge accounting model for a recognised foreign-currency receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions, including certain highly probable intragroup foreign-currency transactions in consolidated financial statements, as possible hedged items.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 model for hedging foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation at the consolidated level.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency translation accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements, which is directly relevant to the underlying FX exposure.
